meta.description: FRCS (SN) revision source list — FRCS Specialty Examination in Surgical Neurology (UK)

section_1.format: Written — Single Best Answer (2h) + Extended Matching Items (2h 30m), same day

section_2.purpose: Clinical knowledge and judgement at day-one consultant level

sources.name: JCIE Neurosurgery — Official Exam Page
sources.notes: Official exam body page. Contains downloadable regulations, Syllabus Blueprint 2023, and sample questions.

sources.name: JCIE Neurosurgery Syllabus Blueprint 2023 (PDF)
sources.notes: Downloadable from JCIE neurosurgery page. Defines exam topic areas and weightings. Download manually and save to guidelines/.

sources.name: JCIE Neurosurgery Sample Questions (PDF)
sources.notes: Official sample SBA and EMI questions from JCIE. Essential for understanding question style. Download manually.

sources.name: SBNS — Examinations Page
sources.notes: SBNS links to exam body, EANS alternative route, and position statements relevant to viva.

sources.name: SBNS — Training Resources for Neurosurgery Trainees
sources.notes: Lists eBrain, GAIN webinars, Brain School, neuroanatomy courses, simulation courses.

sources.name: BNTA — British Neurosurgical Trainees' Association: FRCS (SN) Page
sources.notes: Peer-curated reading list, exam tips, and links compiled by UK neurosurgery trainees.

sources.name: eBrain — Neuroscience E-Learning Platform
sources.notes: Over 500 modules covering all aspects of neuroscience. Includes virtual patients and question banks. Specifically recommended by SBNS for FRCS preparation.

sources.name: GAIN — Great Britain and Ireland Neurosurgery Teaching Programme (Monthly Webinars)
sources.cost: free (register via SBNS training resources page)
sources.notes: Monthly webinars: 2 x 2-hour sessions, last Wednesday and Thursday of each month (5–7 pm). Covers national neurosurgery curriculum at FRCS exam level.

sources.name: Brain School — Monthly Neurosurgery Seminars
sources.cost: free (hybrid: online + in-person at RCS England)
sources.notes: Monthly seminars last Wednesday at 7pm. Hybrid format at Royal College of Surgeons of England. Register via SBNS.

sources.name: FRCS Companion — Revision and Mock Exam Platform
sources.notes: Online question bank and mock exam resource for FRCS. Verify neurosurgery specialty coverage before subscribing.

sources.name: Neurocourses — King's College London Neuroanatomy
sources.notes: Neuroanatomy teaching from King's College London. Recommended by SBNS for FRCS preparation.

sources.name: CLNA — Cambridge Lectures in Neurosurgical Anatomy
sources.notes: Dedicated neurosurgical anatomy lectures from Cambridge. Recommended by SBNS.

sources.name: Radiopaedia — Neuroradiology Reference
sources.notes: Gold-standard free neuroradiology reference. Essential for imaging findings tested in Section 2 viva. Peer-reviewed by radiologists and clinicians.

sources.name: NICE NG232 — Head Injury: Assessment and Early Management (2023)
sources.notes: Replaces CG176. 2023 update. Covers TBI triage, CT head rules (NICE criteria), transfer, ICP monitoring, neurosurgical referral thresholds.

sources.name: NICE NG228 — Subarachnoid Haemorrhage: Diagnosis and Management (2022)
sources.notes: Covers diagnosis (CT, LP, CTA), grading, coiling vs clipping, vasospasm management (nimodipine), hydrocephalus, re-bleeding prevention.

sources.name: NICE NG99 — Brain Tumours (Primary) and Brain Metastases in Over 16s (2018, updated 2021)
sources.notes: Covers diagnosis, MDT referral, surgery, radiotherapy, temozolomide, surveillance, palliative care. Includes gliomas, meningiomas, and brain metastases.

sources.name: NICE NG217 — Epilepsies in Children, Young People and Adults (2022)
sources.notes: Essential for surgical epilepsy viva topics: resective surgery referral criteria, pre-surgical evaluation, VNS, corpus callosotomy.

sources.name: NICE NG234 — Spinal Metastases and Metastatic Spinal Cord Compression (2023)
sources.notes: MSCC pathway: recognition, MRI urgency, dexamethasone, surgery vs radiotherapy, SINS score, rehabilitation.

sources.name: NICE NG127 — Suspected Neurological Conditions: Recognition and Referral (2019, updated 2023)
sources.notes: Referral thresholds for symptoms indicating neurological conditions — relevant for outpatient/clinic viva scenarios.

sources.name: National Clinical Guideline for Stroke for the UK and Ireland (2023, 6th ed) — RCP/SIGN/RCPI
sources.notes: Definitive UK/Ireland stroke guideline endorsed by RCP, SIGN and RCPI. Covers ICH surgical management, thrombolysis, thrombectomy, carotid surgery, SAH. Explicitly cited in BNTA FRCS reading list. Over half the recommendations are new/updated in 2023.

sources.name: Society for Endocrinology — Consensus Guideline on Pituitary Apoplexy (2024, Nature Reviews Endocrinology)
sources.notes: 2024 update. Explicitly cited in BNTA FRCS reading list. Covers diagnosis, hydrocortisone replacement, surgical vs conservative management, Pituitary Apoplexy Score (PAS). Most current version available.

sources.name: WHO Classification of Tumours of the CNS (5th ed, 2021) — Summary Access
sources.cost: ~£70 (print); key classification also summarised in free Acta Neuropathologica articles
sources.notes: Definitive CNS tumour classification — now integrates molecular markers (IDH, 1p/19q, MGMT, TERT, H3). Critical for neuro-oncology exam questions. Free summaries via PubMed/PMC.

sources.name: Greenberg — Handbook of Neurosurgery (10th ed)
sources.notes: Single most-referenced neurosurgery handbook for FRCS. Comprehensive, well-indexed, evidence-based. Both print and e-book available. Explicitly cited by BNTA.

sources.name: Samandouras — The Neurosurgeon's Handbook (Oxford University Press)
sources.notes: UK-perspective alternative to Greenberg. BNTA notes it is 'more readable'. Particularly good for viva preparation.

sources.name: Elwell, Kirollos et al — Neurosurgery: The Essential Guide to the Oral and Clinical Exam (2nd ed, 2022)
sources.notes: Written specifically for the FRCS (SN) oral/clinical exam by UK neurosurgeons. Covers clinical knowledge, investigation interpretation, consent, treatment options. Includes 72 online revision flashcards.

sources.name: Johnson & Green — Landmark Papers in Neurosurgery (Oxford)
sources.notes: Explicitly cited in BNTA reading list. Summarises key RCTs and landmark studies (ISAT, ISUIA, SPORT, STICH, etc.) that examiners expect candidates to know.

sources.name: Young, Young & Tolbert — Basic Clinical Neuroscience (3rd ed)
sources.notes: Recommended by BNTA for neuroscience foundations (neuroanatomy, neurophysiology, neuropathology basics).

sources.name: Manter & Gatz — Essentials of Clinical Neuroanatomy and Neurophysiology (10th ed)
sources.notes: Recommended by BNTA. Concise neuroanatomy and neurophysiology — useful for Section 1 neuroscience questions.

sources.name: Behind The Knife — Surgery Podcast (Neurosurgery Episodes)
sources.notes: US surgical podcast — cited in BNTA list for broad surgical overview. Not UK-specific; toggle on if useful.

sources.name: Queen Square Neurosurgery Review Course (Annual, London)
sources.notes: Annual intensive revision course at Queen Square, London (UCLH). Check for current year dates and booking.

sources.name: Aberdeen FRCS (SN) Viva Course
sources.notes: Viva practice course specifically for FRCS (SN). Recommended by BNTA approximately one month before exam.

sources.name: Liverpool Neurosurgical Masterclass (2.5-year rolling programme)
sources.notes: 2.5-year rolling FRCS programme covering: Skull Base & Vascular, Neuro-Oncology, Spine, Trauma & Hydrocephalus, Functional Neurosurgery.
